intro: Achras parvifolia, commonly known as the Castor Oil Plant or the Borneo Croton, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. The health benefits of Achras parvifolia include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in bioactive constituents, including ricinoleic acid, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
